profile.behavior: Forages mainly on or near the ground for small seeds and insects; often uses low shrubs or other exposed perches.
field_marks: Small, slender sparrow with a rounded head and relatively long tail
Pale gray face with a crisp white eye-ring
Warm rufous crown and rufous line behind the eye
Short, conical pink bill; legs are pinkish
Brown, black-streaked back with two faint pale wingbars
Pale gray-buff underparts and a subtly notched tail
profile.habitat: Open, early-successional country: grassy old fields, pastures, brushy edges, fencerows, and woodland openings with scattered shrubs or small trees for perches.

sources.title: Field Sparrow Identification — All About Birds

findings: Independent verification confirms Field Sparrow (Spizella pusilla), family Passerellidae, and the stated length (12–15 cm), weight (11–15 g), and wingspan (19–22 cm).

All visible factual claims are consistent with the verified sources: small slender build, rounded head, relatively long subtly notched tail, pale gray face, white eye-ring, rufous crown and postocular line, short pink conical bill, pinkish legs, black-streaked brown back, weak paired wingbars, and pale gray-buff underparts.

The painted bird agrees closely with the supplied field-reference photos in facial pattern, pink bill and legs, warm rufous crown, streaked upperparts, wingbars, and long tail. It depicts one head, one beak, two legs, one tail, and two wings (one exposed and one occluded in side view); the separate wing study is an anatomical detail, not an added body part.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.
